text: This book offers a systematic approach to understanding the war between Russia and Ukraine by using the principle of 'resilient fighting power' to assess the operational effectiveness of Russian and Ukrainian armed forces over the first year of the war, which started on 24 February 2022. The war started in 2014 and lasted for eight years until the full-scale invasion by Russia in 24 February 2022. It is not a new war but one that has reignited the debate on modern inter-state warfighting, which has not been seen in Europe for many decades. This book is not a history of the operational events of the war in Ukraine, but an initial systematic analysis of several aspects of the war in different domains using the principle of fighting power to gauge the operational performance of armed forces. Firstly, the book discusses the conceptual underpinning and post-Cold War evolution of the Soviet legacy strategic doctrine in the Ukrainian and Russian armed forces. Secondly, the book offers an assessment of the developments and events in the different domains of warfighting in the ground, air, sea, and cyber wars. Thirdly, the book analyses the contribution of foreign support, including aid, and the supply of weapons to the Ukrainian armed forces' resilience in the war against Russia. Finally, the book ends by offering some general remarks on inter-state war in today's global security environment and on warfare in the coming years. This book will be of interest to scholars and researchers in military and strategic studies, defence studies, foreign policy, Russian studies and international relations. The Open Access version of this book available at http://www.taylorfrancis.com has been published under a Creative Commons Attribution-Non Commercial-No Derivatives 4.0 license.
